semanticCore: Guiding, presenting a gift, or showing the path
primaryMeaning: To guide or direct to the path
quranicRelevance: Central concept of divine guidance (Huda). It refers to God guiding humanity to truth, the prophets as guides, the Quran as a book of guidance, and also describes the sacrificial gift/offering (Hady) sent to the Sacred House.

keyDerivations.meaning: To guide, direct, or show the way
Sacrificial animals offered at the Kaaba
To be guided, find the way, or accept guidance

figurativeOrRareUsages: هَادِيَةٌ: The leading parts of a herd, or the neck of a horse (from leading).
اهْتَدَى (Form VIII): To find the way home or to a destination.
discardedNoise: Obscure descriptions of horse movements or paths in the desert.
Dialectal variations of (هَدِيَّة) in pre-Islamic poetry.
summaryEn: The root signifies guiding and directing, as well as the concept of offering a gift, which Quranically manifests as divine guidance and ritual sacrificial offerings.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الهداية والإرشاد، وكذلك على تقديم الهدية، وهو ما يتجلى في القرآن في الهداية الإلهية والهدي المهدى إلى البيت الحرام.
rawText: أَهْدَى مِنْ دُعَيْمِيصِ الرَّمْلِ More expert, &c: see art. دعمص. أَهْدَىَ 4 see 1. المَهْدِىُّ , meaning The directed by God to the truth, is a proper name, and the name of him of whose coming at the end of time the happy tidings have been announced. (TA.) [It is always so pronounced by the Arabs in the present day: not المُهْدِى.] اِهْتَدَى 8 He became rightly directed; followed a right direction; (K;) went aright; as also 🡻هَدَى. (S.) === He guided himself. === He went a right way: went aright. === لَا يَهْتَدِى إِلَى جِهَةٍ He cannot go aright: or knows not the way that he would pursue; or knows not in what direction to go: sometimes said of a drunken man. === لَا يَهْتَدِى لِأَمْرِهِ means He does not, or cannot, find the way to accomplish, or perform, his affair. === اِهْتَدَى He found, (MA,) or took (KL,) the right way or road. (MA, KL.) === دَاهِيَةُ لاَ يُهْتَدَى لَهَا, by which دَاهِيَةُ الغَبَرِ is expl. in the S and O, means لَا يُهْتَدَى للَّنَّجَآءِ مِنْهَا, by which the same phrase is expl. in the JK: or it may be well rendered A calamity in relation to which one knows not the right course to pursue. === اِهْتَدَى also signifies He continued to be rightly directed, or to follow a right dirertion: and he sought to be rightly directed, or to follow a right direction. (TA.) === اِهَدَّى and اِهِدِّى, for اِهْتَدَى; like اِعَذَّرَ and اِعِذِّرَ, for اِعْتَذَرَ. تَهَادَنَا 6 They (two parties who had been at war) made a truce, each with the other. (T, art. نبذ.) هَادٍ : see an ex. of its pl. هَوَادِى meaning Necks of horses, voce تَالٍ. === هَادِيَةٌ The fore part of the neck of a horse. (K in art. سلف.) === أَخَذَ هَادِىَ الرَّحَى فَجَعَلَ يُدِيرُهَا [He took the handle of the mill, and begun to turn it]. (K, art. خبز.) هَدَاهُ 1 He directed him, or guided him, to the way; (K, * TA;) directed him aright; or caused him to take, or follow, a right way or course or direction. (K, TA.) See 8. === هَدَى العَرُوسَ He sent [or conducted] the bride (MA, KL) to her husband, (MA,) or to the house of her husband: (KL;) i. q. زَفَّهَا, (K in art. زف,) and so 🡻أَهْدَاهَا. (Msb in that art.) === يَهْدِى meaning يُؤَدِّى: see an ex. in a verse cited voce طَبَعٌ. هَدِيَّةٌ [n. un. of هَدِىٌّ] A present; i. e. a thing sent to another in token of courtesy or honour: (Msb;) such as is termed طَرِيفٌ and لَطَفٌ. (JK.) === 🡻هَدْىٌ and هَدِىٌّ [coll. gen. ns.] What one brings as an offering to Mekkeh, (K,) or to the Kaabeh, (Beyd, v. 2,) or to the Haram, (S, Mgh,) consisting of camels (Lth, S, Mgh, Msb) or other beasts, (Lth,) namely kine or sheep or goats, (Mgh,) to be sacrificed, (TA,) and of goods or commodities: (Lth:) n. un. with ة. (S, &c.) === Also, Camels, absolutely. (TA.) === هَدِىٌّ also One who is entitled to respect, or honour, or protection: so in a verse cited voce اِسْتَبَآءَ. (ISk in T in art. بوأ.) هَدْىٌ A way, course, method, mode, or manner, of acting, or conduct, or proceeding, or the like; (Msb, K;) as also 🡻هِدْيَةٌ and 🡻هَدْيَةٌ: (K:) or to the second and third: and the first is pl. [or coll. gen. n.] of the last: (S:) and a god way, &c.: and calm, or placid, deportment; or calmness, or placidity, of deportment: (TA:) see also دَلٌ. === هَدْىٌ [Conduct, mode of life; manners]. ___ See هَدِىٌّ. هُوَ عَلَى هُدًى He is following, or he follows, a right direction. === الهُدَى The Kur-án. (Bd, Jel in lxxii. 13, &c.) هِدْيَةٌ and هَدْيَةٌ: see هَدْيٌ.
